title: 〜いかんだ / いかんにかかわらず (depending on / regardless of)
pattern: N + のいかんだ / のいかんにかかわらず
meaning: Depending on the state of / regardless of — very formal bureaucratic expression
explanation: いかん is a formal/written word meaning 'what the situation is' or 'how things are'. のいかんだ means 'it depends on how X turns out'. のいかんにかかわらず means 'regardless of the state of X'. Both are formal/bureaucratic.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"結果のいかんにかかわらず、全力を尽くします。","reading":"けっかのいかんにかかわらず、ぜんりょくをつくします。","english":"Regardless of the result, I will give my all."},{"japanese":"対応のいかんによっては、法的措置を取ります。","reading":"たいおうのいかんによっては、ほうてきそちをとります。","english":"Depending on the response, legal measures will be taken."},{"japanese":"理由のいかんを問わず、遅刻は禁止です。","reading":"りゆうのいかんをとわず、ちこくはきんしです。","english":"Regardless of the reason, being late is prohibited."}]

title: 〜まじ (must not — literary prohibition)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + まじ / まじき + N
meaning: Must not / should not — literary/archaic prohibition
explanation: まじ is a classical auxiliary expressing prohibition or strong negative intention. まじき is the attributive form. Found in literary texts, formal writing, and set expressions. The modern equivalent is べきではない.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"教師としてあるまじき行為だ。","reading":"きょうしとしてあるまじきこういだ。","english":"It is conduct unbefitting a teacher."},{"japanese":"人として許すまじき行為だ。","reading":"ひととしてゆるすまじきこういだ。","english":"It is conduct that must not be forgiven as a human being."},{"japanese":"その秘密は明かすまじ。","reading":"そのひみつはあかすまじ。","english":"That secret must not be revealed."}]

title: 〜べく (in order to — formal/literary purpose)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + べく
meaning: In order to / with the aim of — formal/literary purpose marker
explanation: べく is the literary/classical equivalent of ために for expressing purpose. It is followed by an action the subject takes in pursuit of the goal. More literary than ために; found in news, formal essays, and classical-influenced writing.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"夢を実現すべく、毎日努力しています。","reading":"ゆめをじつげんすべく、まいにちどりょくしています。","english":"In order to realise my dream, I work hard every day."},{"japanese":"問題を解決すべく、会議が開かれました。","reading":"もんだいをかいけつすべく、かいぎがひらかれました。","english":"A meeting was held in order to resolve the problem."},{"japanese":"合格すべく、必死で勉強しました。","reading":"ごうかくすべく、ひっしでべんきょうしました。","english":"I studied desperately in order to pass."}]

title: 〜んがため(に) (for the purpose of — literary)
pattern: V-ない stem + んがために / んがための + N
meaning: For the purpose of — archaic/literary purpose expression
explanation: んがため(に) is a classical grammar pattern expressing strong purpose. ん is the archaic negative auxiliary form serving as a volitional here. Very literary and not used in modern conversation; appears in formal essays, historical texts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"勝たんがために、あらゆる努力をした。","reading":"かたんがために、あらゆるどりょくをした。","english":"For the purpose of winning, every effort was made."},{"japanese":"生き残らんがための戦いが続いた。","reading":"いきのこらんがためのたたかいがつづいた。","english":"The struggle for the purpose of survival continued."},{"japanese":"真実を知らんがため、彼は危険を冒した。","reading":"しんじつをしらんがため、かれはきけんをおかした。","english":"For the purpose of knowing the truth, he took risks."}]

title: 〜んばかりに (as if about to / so much that it seems)
pattern: V-ない stem + んばかりに
meaning: As if about to / seemingly on the verge of — extreme degree or appearance
explanation: んばかりに expresses that something is on the verge of happening or looks as if it is about to. It describes an extreme state or appearance. The ん is classical (related to む). Often describes emotional or physical extremity.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"泣かんばかりに、頼み込みました。","reading":"なかんばかりに、たのみこみました。","english":"He pleaded as if he were about to cry."},{"japanese":"爆発せんばかりの怒りを抑えた。","reading":"ばくはつせんばかりのいかりをおさえた。","english":"He suppressed anger that was on the verge of exploding."},{"japanese":"溢れんばかりの笑顔で迎えてくれました。","reading":"あふれんばかりのえがおでむかえてくれました。","english":"She greeted us with a smile so big it seemed to overflow."}]

title: 〜かと思いきや (contrary to expectation, ...)
pattern: S (plain) + かと思いきや
meaning: Just when you thought / contrary to expectation — surprising reversal
explanation: かと思いきや expresses a sudden reversal of expectation. The speaker thought X was the case, but then Y (the opposite or something surprising) happened. Always followed by an unexpected outcome. Conversational and slightly literary.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"試験は難しいかと思いきや、意外と簡単だった。","reading":"しけんはむずかしいかとおもいきや、いがいとかんたんだった。","english":"Just when I thought the exam would be hard, it was surprisingly easy."},{"japanese":"雨かと思いきや、急に晴れてきた。","reading":"あめかとおもいきや、きゅうにはれてきた。","english":"Just when I thought it would rain, it suddenly cleared up."},{"japanese":"怒っているかと思いきや、笑い出した。","reading":"おこっているかとおもいきや、わらいだした。","english":"Just when I thought he was angry, he burst out laughing."}]

title: 〜ならでは (unique to / only possible with)
pattern: N + ならでは (の + N / できない)
meaning: Unique to / only possible with — something is special and exclusive to X
explanation: ならでは expresses that something is uniquely characteristic of X and could only be found there or done by X. ならではの + N is the attributive form. Carries a positive, admiring tone.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"京都ならではの風景が楽しめます。","reading":"きょうとならではのふうけいがたのしめます。","english":"You can enjoy scenery unique to Kyoto."},{"japanese":"プロならではの技術に驚きました。","reading":"プロならではのぎじゅつにおどろきました。","english":"I was amazed by the skill unique to a professional."},{"japanese":"日本ならでは、こんな細かいサービスはできない。","reading":"にほんならでは、こんなこまかいサービスはできない。","english":"Such detailed service is only possible in Japan."}]

title: 〜ともなると / ともなれば (when it comes to that level)
pattern: N + ともなると / ともなれば
meaning: When it comes to / at the level of — marks an elevated status that entails certain expectations
explanation: ともなると/ともなれば expresses that when someone or something reaches a certain level or status, certain things naturally follow. It implies that at that level, the associated consequences, duties, or characteristics are expected.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"社長ともなると、責任が大きいですね。","reading":"しゃちょうともなると、せきにんがおおきいですね。","english":"When it comes to being a company president, the responsibility is great."},{"japanese":"プロともなれば、そのくらいできて当然だ。","reading":"プロともなれば、そのくらいできてとうぜんだ。","english":"When you're a professional, being able to do that much is only natural."},{"japanese":"有名人ともなると、プライバシーがなくなる。","reading":"ゆうめいじんともなると、プライバシーがなくなる。","english":"When you become a celebrity, you lose your privacy."}]

title: 〜ともなく / ともなしに (without particular intent)
pattern: V (plain) + ともなく / ともなしに
meaning: Without particularly intending to — doing something vaguely or absent-mindedly
explanation: ともなく/ともなしに expresses that an action is done without clear intent or purpose, often absent-mindedly. Common patterns: 誰ともなく (from no one in particular), 見るともなく見る (gaze without really looking). Literary.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"聞くともなく、隣の会話が耳に入った。","reading":"きくともなく、となりのかいわがみにはいった。","english":"Without particularly listening, the conversation next to me entered my ears."},{"japanese":"見るともなく窓の外を眺めていた。","reading":"みるともなくまどのそとをながめていた。","english":"I was gazing outside the window without particularly looking."},{"japanese":"誰ともなく拍手が起こりました。","reading":"だれともなくはくしゅがおこりました。","english":"Applause arose from no one in particular."}]

title: 〜なしに(は) (without / cannot do without)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + ことなしに / N + なしに(は)
meaning: Without — marks an indispensable condition; something cannot be done without X
explanation: なしに marks a condition without which something is impossible. なしには makes it conditional: 'without X, it is not possible'. More emphatic than ないで. Often used in formal or rhetorical contexts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"準備なしには、成功はあり得ない。","reading":"じゅんびなしには、せいこうはありえない。","english":"Without preparation, success is impossible."},{"japanese":"彼の協力なしには、このプロジェクトは完成しなかった。","reading":"かれのきょうりょくなしには、このプロジェクトはかんせいしなかった。","english":"Without his cooperation, this project would not have been completed."},{"japanese":"苦労なしに得た成功は、長続きしないものだ。","reading":"くろうなしにえたせいこうは、ながつづきしないものだ。","english":"Success obtained without hardship does not last."}]

title: 〜にして (at the stage of / being — formal emphasis)
pattern: N + にして
meaning: At the level/stage of / being that — marks an impressive stage or simultaneous quality
explanation: にして has several literary uses: (1) 'at the level/age of' (emphasising a remarkable achievement at that stage); (2) 'being both X and Y' (〜にして〜でもある); (3) 'and yet / even for'. All are formal.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"30歳にして、すでに世界的な芸術家だ。","reading":"30さいにして、すでにせかいてきなげいじゅつかだ。","english":"At the age of 30, he is already a world-renowned artist."},{"japanese":"彼は科学者にして哲学者でもある。","reading":"かれはかがくしゃにしてていがくしゃでもある。","english":"He is both a scientist and a philosopher."},{"japanese":"ベテランにしてなお、失敗することがある。","reading":"ベテランにしてなお、しっぱいすることがある。","english":"Even for a veteran, there are still times of failure."}]

title: 〜において / における (at / in — formal location/context)
pattern: N + において / における + N
meaning: At / in / in the context of — formal location or field of activity
explanation: において is the formal/written equivalent of で for marking location or field. における is the attributive form. Very common in academic writing, business documents, and formal speech. The casual equivalent is で.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"グローバル化が進む現代において、英語力は不可欠です。","reading":"グローバルかがすすむげんだいにおいて、えいごりょくはふかけつです。","english":"In the modern era of advancing globalisation, English ability is indispensable."},{"japanese":"教育における格差は大きな問題です。","reading":"きょういくにおけるかくさはおおきなもんだいです。","english":"Disparity in education is a major problem."},{"japanese":"スポーツの世界においても、メンタルは重要です。","reading":"スポーツのせかいにおいても、メンタルはじゅうようです。","english":"Even in the world of sports, mental strength is important."}]

title: 〜に即して (in accordance with / strictly following)
pattern: N + に即して / に即した + N
meaning: In strict accordance with / closely following — acts or evaluates exactly according to a standard
explanation: に即して means to act in strict accordance with or closely following something (rules, reality, facts). に即した is the attributive form. Slightly different from に沿って (which implies going along a path/direction); に即して implies strict conformity.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"現実に即した解決策が必要です。","reading":"げんじつにそくしたかいけつさくがひつようです。","english":"A solution that closely follows reality is necessary."},{"japanese":"事実に即して報告してください。","reading":"じじつにそくしてほうこくしてください。","english":"Please report in strict accordance with the facts."},{"japanese":"法律に即した対応が求められます。","reading":"ほうりつにそくしたたいおうがもとめられます。","english":"A response in strict accordance with the law is required."}]

title: 〜をおいてほかにない (there is no one/nothing but)
pattern: N + をおいて(ほかに)ない
meaning: There is no one / nothing other than — emphatic assertion of uniqueness
explanation: をおいてほかにない asserts that there is nothing or no one else that can fill the role or match the quality. It is emphatic and positive. More literary and formal than 〜しかない.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この役を演じられるのは彼をおいてほかにない。","reading":"このやくをえんじられるのはかれをおいてほかにない。","english":"There is no one other than him who can play this role."},{"japanese":"今の状況を打開できるのは、彼女をおいてほかにいない。","reading":"いまのじょうきょうをだかいできるのは、かのじょをおいてほかにいない。","english":"There is no one other than her who can break through the current situation."},{"japanese":"この計画を実現できるのは、我々をおいてほかにない。","reading":"このけいかくをじつげんできるのは、われわれをおいてほかにない。","english":"There is no one other than us who can realise this plan."}]

title: 〜をよそに (ignoring / indifferent to)
pattern: N + をよそに
meaning: Ignoring / indifferent to — proceeds despite or regardless of someone's concern/situation
explanation: をよそに expresses that someone carries on with their own affairs while ignoring or being indifferent to the situation or feelings of others nearby. Often carries a critical nuance — the person is insensitively disregarding others.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"親の心配をよそに、子供は平気な顔をしていた。","reading":"おやのしんぱいをよそに、こどもはへいきなかおをしていた。","english":"Ignoring their parents' worries, the child looked unconcerned."},{"japanese":"周囲の反対をよそに、彼は計画を進めた。","reading":"しゅういのはんたいをよそに、かれはけいかくをすすめた。","english":"Indifferent to the opposition around him, he pushed the plan forward."},{"japanese":"世間の批判をよそに、彼らは活動を続けた。","reading":"せけんのひはんをよそに、かれらはかつどうをつづけた。","english":"Ignoring society's criticism, they continued their activities."}]

title: 〜ずにはいられない (cannot help but — involuntary feeling)
pattern: V-ない stem + ずにはいられない
meaning: Cannot help but / can't resist — involuntary feeling or action
explanation: ずにはいられない expresses an action that the speaker cannot help but do — it is an uncontrollable urge or feeling. More literary than ないではいられない. する becomes せずにはいられない.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この映画を見ると、泣かずにはいられない。","reading":"このえいがをみると、なかずにはいられない。","english":"When I watch this movie, I can't help but cry."},{"japanese":"そんな不条理を見ると、怒らずにはいられない。","reading":"そんなふじょうりをみると、おこらずにはいられない。","english":"Seeing such injustice, I can't help but get angry."},{"japanese":"彼女の笑顔を見ると、微笑まずにはいられない。","reading":"かのじょのえがおをみると、ほほえまずにはいられない。","english":"When I see her smile, I can't help but smile too."}]

title: 〜まい (probably not / will not — volitional negative)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + まい / するまい / くるまい
meaning: Probably not / definitely will not — negative conjecture or strong negative intention
explanation: まい is a classical auxiliary expressing negative conjecture (probably won't) or strong negative volitional intent (I will not). Group 1 verbs: plain form + まい; Group 2: drop る + まい or plain + まい; する → するまい; くる → くるまい. Formal/literary.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"あの人はもう二度と来るまいと思います。","reading":"あのひとはもうにどとくるまいとおもいます。","english":"I think that person will probably not come again."},{"japanese":"二度とこんな失敗はすまいと決意した。","reading":"にどとこんなしっぱいはすまいとけついした。","english":"I resolved that I would never make such a mistake again."},{"japanese":"彼はそんなことをするまいと思っていたが、した。","reading":"かれはそんなことをするまいとおもっていたが、した。","english":"I thought he probably wouldn't do such a thing, but he did."}]

title: 〜や否や (as soon as — literary)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + や否や
meaning: The moment / as soon as — literary expression for immediate succession
explanation: や否や is a literary expression meaning 'the very moment X happened, Y happened immediately'. It emphasises the instantaneous nature of the succession. More dramatic and literary than とたんに or たとたん. The second action happens before the first is fully complete.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"チャイムが鳴るや否や、生徒たちは飛び出した。","reading":"チャイムがなるやいなや、せいとたちはとびだした。","english":"The very moment the bell rang, the students burst out."},{"japanese":"彼は名前を呼ばれるや否や、立ち上がった。","reading":"かれはなまえをよばれるやいなや、たちあがった。","english":"The moment his name was called, he stood up."},{"japanese":"目が覚めるや否や、彼女は走り出した。","reading":"めがさめるやいなや、かのじょははしりだした。","english":"The very moment she woke up, she started running."}]

title: 〜が早いか (as soon as — with urgency)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + が早いか
meaning: No sooner ... than / the instant — extremely rapid succession, often physical action
explanation: が早いか describes an action that happens with extreme immediacy — no sooner than X happened, Y already happened. More emphatic than や否や and typically describes rapid physical actions. Literary.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"ドアが開くが早いか、彼は飛び出した。","reading":"ドアがあくがはやいか、かれはとびだした。","english":"No sooner did the door open than he jumped out."},{"japanese":"授業が終わるが早いか、生徒たちは帰り始めた。","reading":"じゅぎょうがおわるがはやいか、せいとたちはかえりはじめた。","english":"No sooner did the class end than students began leaving."},{"japanese":"電話が鳴るが早いか、彼女は飛びついた。","reading":"でんわがなるがはやいか、かのじょはとびついた。","english":"No sooner did the phone ring than she grabbed it."}]

title: 〜ものを (if only / could have — regret)
pattern: V-た / Adj (plain) + ものを
meaning: If only / could have — regret that something should have been different
explanation: ものを expresses regret or complaint that things didn't go as they should have. The ものを clause states what should have been done, but wasn't. Carries strong emotional weight — frustration or lament. More formal and literary than のに.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"もっと早く言ってくれればよかったものを。","reading":"もっとはやくいってくれればよかったものを。","english":"If only you had told me sooner."},{"japanese":"素直に謝ればよかったものを、意地を張ってしまった。","reading":"すなおにあやまればよかったものを、いじをはってしまった。","english":"If only he had sincerely apologised, but he was stubborn."},{"japanese":"準備さえしていれば成功したものを。","reading":"じゅんびさえしていればせいこうしたものを。","english":"If only we had prepared, we would have succeeded."}]

title: 〜もさることながら (not to mention / and of course)
pattern: N + もさることながら
meaning: Not to mention / X is a given, but also Y — acknowledges X then adds Y as equally important
explanation: もさることながら acknowledges that X is certainly true or important, but goes on to say Y is also (or even more) important. The first item is taken as a given; the second is the additional point. Formal.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"デザインもさることながら、使い心地も重要です。","reading":"デザインもさることながら、つかいごこちもじゅうようです。","english":"Not to mention the design, usability is also important."},{"japanese":"外見もさることながら、内面が大切だと思います。","reading":"がいけんもさることながら、ないめんがたいせつだとおもいます。","english":"Not to mention appearance, inner character is important."},{"japanese":"費用もさることながら、時間の問題もあります。","reading":"ひようもさることながら、じかんのもんだいもあります。","english":"Not to mention the cost, there is also the issue of time."}]

title: 〜たる(もの) (for one who is — formal expectation)
pattern: N + たる + N / たるもの
meaning: For one who is / as a [status] — formal assertion of the standards expected of a role
explanation: たる is the classical attributive copula. N + たるもの = 'for something/someone that is N, the following is expected'. It asserts that a certain standard is required by virtue of one's role or status. Formal and literary.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"教師たる者、常に学び続けるべきだ。","reading":"きょうしたるもの、つねにまなびつづけるべきだ。","english":"For one who is a teacher, one should always keep learning."},{"japanese":"リーダーたる者は、責任から逃げてはならない。","reading":"リーダーたるものは、せきにんからにげてはならない。","english":"For one who is a leader, one must not run from responsibility."},{"japanese":"社会人たる者、礼儀を知るべきだ。","reading":"しゃかいじんたるもの、れいぎをしるべきだ。","english":"For one who is a member of society, one should know proper manners."}]

title: 〜たりとも (not even one / even a single)
pattern: 数量 + たりとも + negative
meaning: Not even a single / not one — extreme emphasis on zero tolerance
explanation: たりとも follows a small quantity (一人, 一秒, 一円) to emphasise absolute zero: 'not even one'. Always followed by a negative. Literary and emphatic. Similar to さえ but more absolute.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"一秒たりとも無駄にできない。","reading":"いちびょうたりともむだにできない。","english":"I cannot waste even a single second."},{"japanese":"一人たりとも取り残してはならない。","reading":"ひとりたりともとりのこしてはならない。","english":"Not even a single person must be left behind."},{"japanese":"一円たりとも使わせない。","reading":"いちえんたりともつかわせない。","english":"I won't let them spend even a single yen."}]

title: 〜であれ〜であれ (whether ... or — formal)
pattern: N/V + であれ + N/V + であれ
meaning: Whether ... or — formal expression covering both alternatives
explanation: であれ〜であれ is the formal equivalent of にしても〜にしても or でも〜でも. It states that the result holds regardless of which alternative applies. Found in formal writing, speeches, and literary contexts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"賛成であれ反対であれ、意見を述べてください。","reading":"さんせいであれはんたいであれ、いけんをのべてください。","english":"Whether you agree or disagree, please state your opinion."},{"japanese":"成功であれ失敗であれ、全力を尽くすことが大切だ。","reading":"せいこうであれしっぱいであれ、ぜんりょくをつくすことがたいせつだ。","english":"Whether you succeed or fail, giving your all is important."},{"japanese":"どんな状況であれ、諦めてはいけない。","reading":"どんなじょうきょうであれ、あきらめてはいけない。","english":"Whatever the situation, you must not give up."}]

title: 〜とあって (because / since — special circumstance)
pattern: S (plain) + とあって
meaning: Because / given that — marks a special circumstance that explains an unusual result
explanation: とあって presents a special or notable circumstance that justifies or explains the following result. The circumstance is noteworthy — it explains why something unusual is happening. More formal and specific than から or ので.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"連休とあって、観光地は大変混んでいます。","reading":"れんきゅうとあって、かんこうちはたいへんこんでいます。","english":"Given that it's a long holiday, the tourist spots are very crowded."},{"japanese":"有名アーティストの公演とあって、チケットはすぐに売り切れた。","reading":"ゆうめいアーティストのこうえんとあって、チケットはすぐにうりきれた。","english":"Because it was a performance by a famous artist, tickets sold out immediately."},{"japanese":"初めての海外とあって、彼女は緊張していた。","reading":"はじめてのかいがいとあって、かのじょはきんちょうしていた。","english":"Given that it was her first time abroad, she was nervous."}]

title: 〜とあれば (if it's for / when it comes to — strong motivation)
pattern: S (plain) + とあれば / N + とあれば
meaning: If it's for / when it comes to — strong motivation driving an action
explanation: とあれば expresses that if the condition stated is the case, the speaker is strongly motivated or obligated to act. It implies willingness to go to great lengths for that specific cause.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"あなたのためとあれば、どんなことでもします。","reading":"あなたのためとあれば、どんなことでもします。","english":"If it's for you, I'll do anything."},{"japanese":"命令とあれば、従わなければならない。","reading":"めいれいとあれば、したがわなければならない。","english":"If it's an order, I must comply."},{"japanese":"家族のためとあれば、私は戦うことができます。","reading":"かぞくのためとあれば、わたしはたたかうことができます。","english":"If it's for my family, I am able to fight."}]

title: 〜とばかりに (as if to say / with the attitude of)
pattern: S (plain) + とばかりに
meaning: As if to say / with an attitude of — non-verbal behaviour that implies something
explanation: とばかりに describes an action or attitude that strongly implies a feeling or message, even though it isn't stated in words. Often used to describe facial expressions, gestures, or behaviour that communicates something clearly.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"「どうぞ」とばかりに、彼はドアを大きく開けた。","reading":"「どうぞ」とばかりに、かれはドアをおおきくあけた。","english":"As if to say 'go ahead', he opened the door wide."},{"japanese":"待ってましたとばかりに、彼は立ち上がった。","reading":"まってましたとばかりに、かれはたちあがった。","english":"As if to say 'I've been waiting for this', he stood up."},{"japanese":"違うとばかりに、彼女は首を横に振った。","reading":"ちがうとばかりに、かのじょはくびをよこにふった。","english":"As if to say 'that's wrong', she shook her head."}]

title: 〜とはいえ (even so / that said — concession)
pattern: S (plain) / N + とはいえ
meaning: Even so / that said / although — acknowledges a fact then qualifies it
explanation: とはいえ acknowledges the preceding statement as true, then presents a qualification or contrasting reality. Similar to 'that said' or 'even so' in English. More formal than とはいっても or けど.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"冬とはいえ、今日は暖かいですね。","reading":"ふゆとはいえ、きょうはあたたかいですね。","english":"Even so, it's warm today for winter."},{"japanese":"失敗したとはいえ、挑戦したことは評価すべきだ。","reading":"しっぱいしたとはいえ、ちょうせんしたことはひょうかすべきだ。","english":"Even though he failed, the fact that he tried should be acknowledged."},{"japanese":"小さいとはいえ、機能は十分にあります。","reading":"ちいさいとはいえ、きのうはじゅうぶんにあります。","english":"Even so, despite being small, it has sufficient functionality."}]

title: 〜ではあるまいか (could it be that — formal conjecture)
pattern: S (plain) + ではあるまいか
meaning: Could it be that / perhaps — formal rhetorical question expressing conjecture
explanation: ではあるまいか is a formal rhetorical expression inviting the listener to consider a conjecture. It expresses the speaker's tentative belief as a question. More literary and formal than ではないかと思う. まいか adds uncertainty.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"これが真実ではあるまいか。","reading":"これがしんじつではあるまいか。","english":"Could this perhaps be the truth?"},{"japanese":"彼こそが最適な人物ではあるまいか。","reading":"かれこそがさいてきなじんぶつではあるまいか。","english":"Could it be that he is the most suitable person?"},{"japanese":"その考え方は時代遅れではあるまいか。","reading":"そのかんがえかたはじだいおくれではあるまいか。","english":"Could that way of thinking perhaps be outdated?"}]

title: 〜のみならず (not only — formal)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + のみならず / N + のみならず
meaning: Not only ... but also — formal equivalent of だけでなく
explanation: のみならず is the formal/written equivalent of だけでなく (not only). Found in academic writing, formal speech, and literary contexts. More emphatic and elevated in register than だけでなく.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"その影響は国内のみならず、海外にも及んでいます。","reading":"そのえいきょうはこくないのみならず、かいがいにもおよんでいます。","english":"The impact extends not only domestically but also internationally."},{"japanese":"彼は成績が優秀なのみならず、人柄も素晴らしい。","reading":"かれはせいせきがゆうしゅうなのみならず、ひとがらもすばらしい。","english":"Not only is he academically excellent, but his character is also wonderful."},{"japanese":"環境問題は政府のみならず、個人の問題でもある。","reading":"かんきょうもんだいはせいふのみならず、こじんのもんだいでもある。","english":"Environmental issues are not only a government matter but also a personal one."}]

title: 〜ばこそ (precisely because — emphasis)
pattern: V-ば / Adj-ければ + こそ
meaning: Precisely because — highlights the reason as the essential and exclusive cause
explanation: ばこそ emphasises that X is the precise and exclusive reason for Y. It stresses the indispensability of the reason. More emphatic than から alone. Often expresses that the speaker wants to make the reason explicit.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"あなたが好きなればこそ、こんなことを言うのです。","reading":"あなたがすきなればこそ、こんなことをいうのです。","english":"Precisely because I care about you, I say such things."},{"japanese":"難しければこそ、挑戦する価値がある。","reading":"むずかしければこそ、ちょうせんするかちがある。","english":"Precisely because it is difficult, it is worth challenging."},{"japanese":"信頼しているからこそ、厳しく指導します。","reading":"しんらいしているからこそ、きびしくしどうします。","english":"Precisely because I trust you, I guide you strictly."}]

title: 〜こそあれ (although there is / though)
pattern: V-stem / N + こそあれ
meaning: Although there is / though — acknowledges one aspect while asserting the opposite
explanation: こそあれ is a literary concessive acknowledging one aspect (often a minor or expected quality) while emphasising a contrasting larger truth. It is formal and rarely used in conversation.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"多少の不安こそあれ、自信はあります。","reading":"たしょうのふあんこそあれ、じしんはあります。","english":"Although there is some anxiety, I have confidence."},{"japanese":"困難こそあれ、必ず道はある。","reading":"こんなんこそあれ、かならずみちはある。","english":"Though there are difficulties, there is always a way."},{"japanese":"失敗こそあれ、成長しているのは確かだ。","reading":"しっぱいこそあれ、せいちょうしているのはたしかだ。","english":"Although there are failures, it is certain that growth is happening."}]

title: 〜に至って(は) (now that it has come to / having reached the point)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + に至って / に至っては / に至るまで
meaning: Now that it has come to / having reached — marks an extreme or final point
explanation: に至って expresses that a situation has reached a critical or noteworthy point. に至っては emphasises an extreme or unexpected case within a range. に至るまで = 'up to and including / ranging all the way to'.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"事態がここに至っては、対策を講じるしかない。","reading":"じたいがここにいたっては、たいさくをこうじるしかない。","english":"Now that the situation has come to this, there is nothing to do but take measures."},{"japanese":"子供に至っては、大人より詳しいほどだ。","reading":"こどもにいたっては、おとなよりくわしいほどだ。","english":"Even children know more about it than adults."},{"japanese":"細部に至るまで、丁寧に仕上げました。","reading":"さいぶにいたるまで、ていねいにしあげました。","english":"I finished it carefully, right down to the finest details."}]

title: 〜にとどまらず (not limited to / beyond)
pattern: N / V (plain) + にとどまらず
meaning: Not limited to / extending beyond — marks that something goes further than expected
explanation: にとどまらず expresses that the scope or effect is not confined to X but extends beyond it. Formal equivalent of だけでなく for expressing wider scope or greater extent than stated.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"その問題は一個人にとどまらず、社会全体に関わる。","reading":"そのもんだいはいちこじんにとどまらず、しゃかいぜんたいにかかわる。","english":"That problem is not limited to one individual but involves society as a whole."},{"japanese":"影響は国内にとどまらず、世界に広がっています。","reading":"えいきょうはこくないにとどまらず、せかいにひろがっています。","english":"The impact is not limited to domestic but is spreading worldwide."},{"japanese":"彼の才能はスポーツにとどまらず、芸術にも及ぶ。","reading":"かれのさいのうはスポーツにとどまらず、げいじゅつにもおよぶ。","english":"His talent is not limited to sports but extends to the arts as well."}]

title: 〜にもまして (more than / above all)
pattern: N + にもまして / 何にもまして
meaning: More than / above all — marks something as exceeding even the reference point
explanation: にもまして means 'more than / even more than X'. 何にもまして = 'more than anything else / above all'. It emphasises that the following is at an even higher degree than the comparison point. Formal.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"以前にもまして、努力が必要な時代です。","reading":"いぜんにもまして、どりょくがひつようなじだいです。","english":"More than ever before, it is an era that requires effort."},{"japanese":"何にもまして、健康が一番大切です。","reading":"なににもまして、けんこうがいちばんたいせつです。","english":"Above all else, health is the most important."},{"japanese":"昨年にもまして、今年は忙しいです。","reading":"さくねんにもまして、ことしはいそがしいです。","english":"I am even busier this year than last year."}]

title: 〜こそすれ (far from / if anything — opposite emphasis)
pattern: V-stem + こそすれ + negative conclusion
meaning: If anything / far from — asserts the opposite is more true; the stated action is more likely than its contrary
explanation: こそすれ asserts that if anything, the opposite is happening rather than the negative. It emphasises that X (often a more extreme version) is what is actually occurring. Always followed by a negative or contrary statement. Literary.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"状況は改善こそすれ、悪化はしていない。","reading":"じょうきょうはかいぜんこそすれ、あっかはしていない。","english":"If anything, the situation is improving, not worsening."},{"japanese":"感謝こそすれ、恨む気持ちはありません。","reading":"かんしゃこそすれ、うらむきもちはありません。","english":"If anything, I feel gratitude, not resentment."},{"japanese":"増えこそすれ、減ることはないだろう。","reading":"ふえこそすれ、へることはないだろう。","english":"If anything, it will increase; it will not decrease."}]

title: 〜にせよ〜にせよ (whether ... or — formal)
pattern: N/V + にせよ + N/V + にせよ
meaning: Whether ... or — formal expression covering both alternatives
explanation: にせよ〜にせよ is a slightly less formal alternative to であれ〜であれ for expressing that the result holds regardless of which of two options applies. Also used alone as a concessive: にせよ (even if / even though).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"賛成にせよ反対にせよ、行動が必要です。","reading":"さんせいにせよはんたいにせよ、こうどうがひつようです。","english":"Whether you agree or disagree, action is necessary."},{"japanese":"成功にせよ失敗にせよ、経験になります。","reading":"せいこうにせよしっぱいにせよ、けいけんになります。","english":"Whether success or failure, it becomes an experience."},{"japanese":"どんな結果になるにせよ、後悔しない選択をしたい。","reading":"どんなけっかになるにせよ、こうかいしないせんたくをしたい。","english":"Whatever the result, I want to make a choice I won't regret."}]

title: 〜というものではない (it's not necessarily / not that simple)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + というものではない
meaning: It's not simply the case that / not necessarily — rejects an oversimplified view
explanation: というものではない pushes back against a simplistic or naive assumption. It says that just because X appears true or simple, it doesn't mean the conclusion automatically follows. More emphatic than ではない.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"お金があれば幸せというものではない。","reading":"おかねがあればしあわせというものではない。","english":"Having money doesn't simply mean you're happy."},{"japanese":"努力すれば必ず報われるというものではありません。","reading":"どりょくすればかならずむくわれるというものではありません。","english":"It's not necessarily the case that effort will always be rewarded."},{"japanese":"謝れば全て解決するというものではない。","reading":"あやまればすべてかいけつするというものではない。","english":"It's not as simple as saying that apologising will resolve everything."}]

title: 〜いざしらず / はいざしらず (I don't know about X, but)
pattern: N + はいざしらず / はいざ知らず
meaning: I don't know about X, but / setting X aside — focuses attention away from X onto Y
explanation: はいざしらず (or はいざ知らず) dismisses or sets aside one element (X) to focus on another (Y). It implies: 'I can't speak to X, but Y is certainly the case'. Formal and literary. Often used to contrast a doubtful case with a certain one.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"昔はいざ知らず、今はそんなことは通用しない。","reading":"むかしはいざしらず、いまはそんなことはつうようしない。","english":"I don't know about the past, but such things don't work today."},{"japanese":"専門家はいざしらず、私には難しすぎます。","reading":"せんもんかはいざしらず、わたしにはむずかしすぎます。","english":"Setting aside experts, it is too difficult for me."},{"japanese":"他の人はいざ知らず、私は賛成できません。","reading":"ほかのひとはいざしらず、わたしはさんせいできません。","english":"I don't know about others, but I cannot agree."}]

title: 〜に即した (strictly in accordance with — attributive)
pattern: N + に即した + N
meaning: Strictly in accordance with — attributive form of に即して
explanation: に即した is the attributive (noun-modifying) form of に即して. Used when modifying a noun: 現実に即した計画 = a plan that closely follows reality. More precise than に沿った (which implies following a path).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"現場に即した研修が必要です。","reading":"げんばにそくしたけんしゅうがひつようです。","english":"Training that is in strict accordance with on-the-job reality is needed."},{"japanese":"時代に即した教育改革を進めます。","reading":"じだいにそくしたきょういくかいかくをすすめます。","english":"We will advance educational reform in strict accordance with the times."},{"japanese":"実態に即した政策が求められています。","reading":"じったいにそくしたせいさくがもとめられています。","english":"Policies that strictly follow the actual situation are being called for."}]

title: 〜をして (using / by means of — classical instrumental)
pattern: N + をして + V
meaning: Using / having [someone/something] do — classical causative or instrumental
explanation: をして is a classical/literary construction with two uses: (1) instrumental — by means of / using (〜の口をして言わしめれば); (2) causative — have/make [someone] do (〜をして〜せしめる). Found in classical literature and formal historical writing.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼をして語らしめれば、真実が分かるだろう。","reading":"かれをしてかたらしめれば、しんじつがわかるだろう。","english":"If we have him speak, the truth will become clear."},{"japanese":"神をして嘆かせるほどの悲劇だった。","reading":"かみをしてなげかせるほどのひげきだった。","english":"It was a tragedy so great that even the gods would lament."},{"japanese":"力をして平和を維持することはできない。","reading":"ちからをしてへいわをいじすることはできない。","english":"Peace cannot be maintained by force."}]

title: 〜ずじまい (ended up not doing)
pattern: V-ない stem + ずじまい(だった)
meaning: Ended up not doing — an intended action that was never carried out
explanation: ずじまい expresses that something the speaker intended to do was never actually done. It carries a nuance of regret or resignation. The full form is ずじまいだった (ended up not doing) or ずじまいになった.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"結局、彼に会えずじまいでした。","reading":"けっきょく、かれにあえずじまいでした。","english":"In the end, I never got to meet him."},{"japanese":"買おうと思っていたが、買わずじまいだった。","reading":"かおうとおもっていたが、かわずじまいだった。","english":"I intended to buy it, but ended up not buying it."},{"japanese":"言いたいことがあったが、言えずじまいで終わった。","reading":"いいたいことがあったが、いえずじまいでおわった。","english":"I had something I wanted to say, but ended up not saying it."}]

title: 〜に足る (worthy of / sufficient for)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + に足る / に値する
meaning: Worthy of / sufficient for — something merits the action or consideration
explanation: に足る expresses that something meets the standard required for the action. に値する (to be worth) is closely related. Both are formal. 信頼するに足る = worthy of trust; 検討するに値する = worth considering.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼女は信頼するに足る人物だ。","reading":"かのじょはしんらいするにたるじんぶつだ。","english":"She is a person worthy of trust."},{"japanese":"この作品は高く評価するに足ります。","reading":"このさくひんはたかくひょうかするにたります。","english":"This work is worthy of high evaluation."},{"japanese":"その提案は検討するに値します。","reading":"そのていあんはけんとうするにあたいします。","english":"That proposal is worth considering."}]

title: 〜かたがた (while doing / as well as — formal dual purpose)
pattern: N + かたがた / V-stem + かたがた
meaning: While doing / as well as — doing one thing while also taking the opportunity to do another (formal)
explanation: かたがた expresses doing something while also taking the opportunity to do something else. More formal than ついでに and used in set expressions. お礼かたがた = to express thanks while also... Often used in business or formal correspondence.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"お礼かたがた、ご挨拶に伺いました。","reading":"おれいかたがた、ごあいさつにうかがいました。","english":"I visited both to express my thanks and to pay my respects."},{"japanese":"散歩かたがた、用事を済ませてきました。","reading":"さんぽかたがた、ようじをすませてきました。","english":"I took a walk and also took care of some errands."},{"japanese":"ご報告かたがた、ご相談させていただきます。","reading":"ごほうこくかたがた、ごそうだんさせていただきます。","english":"I would like to both report to you and also consult with you."}]

title: 〜からして (even from / judging even from)
pattern: N + からして
meaning: Even from / judging from — marks a striking starting point that implies more beyond it
explanation: からして marks the most basic or obvious example, implying that even this minimum case supports the conclusion. 'Even from X alone, one can tell Y'. Often carries a mildly critical or emphatic tone.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"声からして、彼は怒っているのが分かった。","reading":"こえからして、かれはおこっているのがわかった。","english":"Even from his voice alone, I could tell he was angry."},{"japanese":"タイトルからして、面白そうな本ですね。","reading":"タイトルからして、おもしろそうなほんですね。","english":"Even just from the title, it seems like an interesting book."},{"japanese":"態度からして、やる気がないのが見え見えだ。","reading":"たいどからして、やるきがないのがみえみえだ。","english":"Even from his attitude alone, it's obvious he has no motivation."}]

title: 〜といったところだ (it's about / at most)
pattern: N / V (plain) + といったところだ
meaning: It's about / at most / something like — gives an approximate or limiting estimate
explanation: といったところだ gives an approximate or maximum estimate, often downplaying expectations. 'It's about X at most' or 'something like X'. Similar to 〜くらいのものだ but more colloquial and common in conversation.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この仕事で稼げるのは月10万円といったところです。","reading":"このしごとでかせげるのはつき10まんえんといったところです。","english":"At most you can earn about 100,000 yen a month from this work."},{"japanese":"メンバーは10人といったところでしょう。","reading":"メンバーは10にんといったところでしょう。","english":"There are about 10 members, give or take."},{"japanese":"今の技術では、その程度といったところが限界だ。","reading":"いまのぎじゅつでは、そのていどといったところがげんかいだ。","english":"With current technology, that level is about the limit."}]

title: 〜かいもなく (despite efforts / despite hopes — futility)
pattern: N の + かいもなく
meaning: Despite efforts / despite hopes — the effort or intention was in vain
explanation: かいもなく expresses that an effort, wish, or care was to no avail. The noun before it is usually the thing that should have helped (努力の甲斐もなく, 祈りの甲斐もなく). Formal and literary; expresses futility with solemnity.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"医者の努力のかいもなく、患者は亡くなりました。","reading":"いしゃのどりょくのかいもなく、かんじゃはなくなりました。","english":"Despite the doctor's efforts, the patient passed away."},{"japanese":"必死の看護のかいもなく、彼は回復しなかった。","reading":"ひっしのかんごのかいもなく、かれはかいふくしなかった。","english":"Despite desperate nursing care, he did not recover."},{"japanese":"祈りのかいもなく、試合に負けてしまった。","reading":"いのりのかいもなく、しあいにまけてしまった。","english":"Despite our prayers, we lost the match."}]

title: 〜手前 (in front of / given that — obligation from position)
pattern: N の + 手前 / V-た + 手前
meaning: In front of / given that — creates obligation due to one's stated position or actions
explanation: 手前 expresses that the speaker is obligated to follow through because of what they said, did, or who they are in front of. The obligation is socially or situationally imposed by the precedent set. Casual: 〜てしまった手前.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"みんなの前で宣言した手前、やり遂げなければならない。","reading":"みんなのまえでせんげんしたてまえ、やりとげなければならない。","english":"Having declared it in front of everyone, I have to follow through."},{"japanese":"上司の手前、文句は言えない。","reading":"じょうしのてまえ、もんくはいえない。","english":"In front of my boss, I can't complain."},{"japanese":"そんな大口を叩いた手前、失敗するわけにはいかない。","reading":"そんなおおぐちをたたいたてまえ、しっぱいするわけにはいかない。","english":"Having boasted so much, I cannot afford to fail."}]

title: 〜ともあろう (for someone as important as)
pattern: N + ともあろう + N (person) + が / ともあろう者が
meaning: For someone of your/their calibre — expresses surprise or reproach at behaviour unbecoming of one's status
explanation: ともあろう expresses that the behaviour described is surprising or inappropriate given the person's status or reputation. It carries reproach or astonishment. 〜ともあろう人が〜するとは = 'for someone of X's calibre to do Y is...'
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"大学教授ともあろう人が、そんなことを言うとは。","reading":"だいがくきょうじゅともあろうひとが、そんなことをいうとは。","english":"For someone of the calibre of a university professor to say such a thing..."},{"japanese":"あなたともあろう人が、なぜそんな失敗をしたのですか。","reading":"あなたともあろうひとが、なぜそんなしっぱいをしたのですか。","english":"Why did someone of your calibre make such a mistake?"},{"japanese":"リーダーともあろうものが、逃げ出すとは情けない。","reading":"リーダーともあろうものが、にげだすとはなさけない。","english":"How pathetic for someone who is supposed to be a leader to run away."}]

title: 〜をもってすれば (if with the power/means of)
pattern: N + をもってすれば
meaning: If with the power/means of — asserts that X is sufficient to achieve the result
explanation: をもってすれば is the conditional form of をもって (by means of). It expresses that if a certain power, ability, or means is applied, the result can be achieved. Formal and emphatic.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"現代の技術をもってすれば、それは実現できる。","reading":"げんだいのぎじゅつをもってすれば、それはじつげんできる。","english":"With modern technology, that can be realised."},{"japanese":"彼の能力をもってすれば、この問題は解決できるはずだ。","reading":"かれののうりょくをもってすれば、このもんだいはかいけつできるはずだ。","english":"With his ability, this problem should be solvable."},{"japanese":"チーム全員の力をもってすれば、不可能はない。","reading":"チームぜんいんのちからをもってすれば、ふかのうはない。","english":"With the power of the entire team, nothing is impossible."}]

title: 〜極まりない / 極まる (extremely / the height of)
pattern: Adj-na stem + 極まりない / 極まる
meaning: Extremely / the height of — formal, strong negative emphasis on an extreme degree
explanation: 極まりない (also 極まる) expresses an extreme degree, usually negative. 非常識極まりない = the height of rudeness/absurdity. Formal and often used in written complaints or formal criticism. ない reinforces the sense of beyond all limits.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"その態度は失礼極まりない。","reading":"そのたいどはしつれいきわまりない。","english":"That attitude is the height of rudeness."},{"japanese":"危険極まりない行為は絶対に禁止です。","reading":"きけんきわまりないこういはぜったいにきんしです。","english":"Acts that are in the extreme of danger are absolutely prohibited."},{"japanese":"そのような不誠実極まる対応には呆れました。","reading":"そのようなふせいじつきわまるたいおうにはあきれました。","english":"I was appalled by such an extremely insincere response."}]

title: 〜ならいざしらず (if X, that's one thing, but)
pattern: N + ならいざ知らず / ならいざしらず
meaning: If it were X, that might be understandable, but — dismisses X as the case and focuses on Y
explanation: ならいざしらず presents X as something that would be understandable or acceptable, then contrasts it with the actual situation (Y) which is not. Used to make a strong contrast between an acceptable case and the unacceptable reality.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"子供ならいざ知らず、大人がそんな言い訳はおかしい。","reading":"こどもならいざしらず、おとながそんないいわけはおかしい。","english":"That excuse might be understandable from a child, but it's strange coming from an adult."},{"japanese":"昔ならいざ知らず、今はそんな方法は通用しません。","reading":"むかしならいざしらず、いまはそんなほうほうはつうようしません。","english":"That method might have worked in the past, but not today."},{"japanese":"初心者ならいざ知らず、プロがこんなミスをするとは。","reading":"しょしんしゃならいざしらず、プロがこんなミスをするとは。","english":"A mistake like this might be expected from a beginner, but from a professional..."}]

title: 〜ものがある (there is an indescribable quality of)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + ものがある
meaning: There is something about it / a certain quality exists — hard-to-articulate impression
explanation: ものがある expresses that there is a notable, hard-to-put-into-words quality about something. The speaker perceives something significant. Formal and literary, used in evaluation or deep observation.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼の演技には何か惹きつけるものがある。","reading":"かれのえんぎにはなにかひきつけるものがある。","english":"There is something captivating about his performance."},{"japanese":"この風景には心を打つものがある。","reading":"このふうけいにはこころをうつものがある。","english":"There is something moving about this scenery."},{"japanese":"彼女の言葉には人を動かすものがある。","reading":"かのじょのことばにはひとをうごかすものがある。","english":"There is something in her words that moves people."}]

title: 〜に至るまで (up to and including / ranging all the way to)
pattern: N から N + に至るまで / N + に至るまで
meaning: All the way up to / ranging to — marks the extreme end of a range
explanation: に至るまで marks the furthest extent of a range, often after から: 'from X all the way to Y'. Used to show comprehensiveness or extremity. The item after に至るまで is the most extreme or unexpected end of the range.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"基礎から応用に至るまで、丁寧に教えます。","reading":"きそからおうようにいたるまで、ていねいにおしえます。","english":"I will teach carefully from the basics all the way to applications."},{"japanese":"日常の小さなことから人生の大きな決断に至るまで、相談してください。","reading":"にちじょうのちいさなことからじんせいのおおきなけつだんにいたるまで、そうだんしてください。","english":"Please consult me on everything from small daily matters all the way to major life decisions."},{"japanese":"あらゆる細部に至るまで確認しました。","reading":"あらゆるさいぶにいたるまでかくにんしました。","english":"I confirmed everything right down to every detail."}]

title: 〜のみか (not only ... but even)
pattern: N / V (plain) + のみか
meaning: Not only ... but even — formal; the second point is even more surprising than the first
explanation: のみか is the formal/literary equivalent of だけでなく, with an added sense that the second element is particularly surprising or extreme. The second element often exceeds expectations more than the first.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"謝らないのみか、相手を責め始めた。","reading":"あやまらないのみか、あいてをせめはじめた。","english":"Not only did he not apologise, but he even started blaming the other person."},{"japanese":"彼女は日本語のみか、中国語と英語も話せる。","reading":"かのじょはにほんごのみか、ちゅうごくごとえいごもはなせる。","english":"Not only Japanese, but she can even speak Chinese and English."},{"japanese":"失敗したのみか、会社に損害まで与えてしまった。","reading":"しっぱいしたのみか、かいしゃにそんがいまであたえてしまった。","english":"Not only did I fail, but I even caused damage to the company."}]

title: 〜とみえて / とみえる (it appears that — inference)
pattern: S (plain) + とみえて / とみえる
meaning: It appears / it seems — inference based on observable evidence
explanation: とみえて and とみえる express an inference based on what the speaker observes. The speaker draws a conclusion from what they can see. Similar to ようで but carries a slightly stronger sense of deduction from visual evidence.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"疲れているとみえて、すぐ眠ってしまいました。","reading":"つかれているとみえて、すぐねむってしまいました。","english":"It appeared that he was tired, as he fell asleep immediately."},{"japanese":"気に入ったとみえて、何度も読み返しています。","reading":"きにいったとみえて、なんどもよみかえしています。","english":"It appears she liked it, as she keeps re-reading it."},{"japanese":"嬉しかったとみえて、顔が輝いていました。","reading":"うれしかったとみえて、かおがかがやいていました。","english":"It appeared she was happy, as her face was glowing."}]

title: 〜そばから (as fast as one does / right after)
pattern: V (plain non-past / た) + そばから
meaning: As fast as / right after doing — what was just done is immediately undone or followed
explanation: そばから expresses that something happens immediately after (and often undoes or contradicts) the preceding action. Carries a slightly frustrated or exasperated tone — 'as soon as I do X, Y already happens'.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"片付けるそばから、子供が散らかす。","reading":"かたづけるそばから、こどもがちらかす。","english":"As fast as I tidy up, the children make a mess again."},{"japanese":"覚えるそばから忘れてしまいます。","reading":"おぼえるそばからわすれてしまいます。","english":"Right after learning something, I forget it."},{"japanese":"教えたそばから、また同じミスをしました。","reading":"おしえたそばから、またおなじミスをしました。","english":"Right after I taught him, he made the same mistake again."}]

title: 〜が最後 (once ... happens, it's all over)
pattern: V-たが最後
meaning: Once that happens, it's all over — an action that sets off an irreversible chain
explanation: たが最後 expresses that once a certain action occurs, something undesirable (and usually irreversible) will follow. It implies a point of no return. Similar in structure to やいなや but always with a negative or irrevocable outcome.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼に話しかけたが最後、1時間は帰れない。","reading":"かれにはなしかけたがさいご、1じかんはかえれない。","english":"Once you start talking to him, you won't be able to leave for an hour."},{"japanese":"その薬を飲んだが最後、もう戻れない。","reading":"そのくすりをのんだがさいご、もうもどれない。","english":"Once you take that medicine, there's no going back."},{"japanese":"彼女に頼まれたが最後、断れなくなってしまう。","reading":"かのじょにたのまれたがさいご、ことわれなくなってしまう。","english":"Once she asks you, you won't be able to refuse."}]

title: 〜ないまでも (if not X, at least Y)
pattern: V-ない + までも
meaning: If not ... at least / even if not fully — a lesser degree is still acceptable or possible
explanation: ないまでも expresses that while the full extent may not be achieved, at least a lesser degree is possible or acceptable. 'Even if not X, at least Y'. Used to present a realistic minimum expectation.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"完璧でないまでも、なんとか形にはなりました。","reading":"かんぺきでないまでも、なんとかかたちにはなりました。","english":"Even if not perfect, it at least took shape somehow."},{"japanese":"毎日でないまでも、週に一度は連絡してください。","reading":"まいにちでないまでも、しゅうにいちどはれんらくしてください。","english":"If not every day, at least contact me once a week."},{"japanese":"理解できないまでも、努力することは大切だ。","reading":"りかいできないまでも、どりょくすることはたいせつだ。","english":"Even if you can't fully understand, it's important to try."}]

title: 〜ようが〜ようが / ようと〜ようと (no matter how / regardless)
pattern: V-volitional + が + V-volitional + が / ようと〜ようと
meaning: No matter how / regardless of what — expresses that the result holds regardless of any effort or variation
explanation: ようが〜ようが (or ようと〜ようと) covers all possibilities of effort or variation, saying the result is the same regardless. More formal and emphatic than ても. Often lists two contrasting options.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"どんなに反対しようが、私は行きます。","reading":"どんなにはんたいしようが、わたしはいきます。","english":"No matter how much you oppose it, I will go."},{"japanese":"雨が降ろうが雪が降ろうが、試合は続けます。","reading":"あめがふろうがゆきがふろうが、しあいはつづけます。","english":"Whether it rains or snows, the match will continue."},{"japanese":"何を言われようと、気にしません。","reading":"なにをいわれようと、きにしません。","english":"No matter what is said, I don't care."}]

title: 〜といわず〜といわず (whether it's ... or ... everywhere/everything)
pattern: N + といわず + N + といわず
meaning: Whether it's ... or ... / everywhere — total coverage without exception
explanation: といわず〜といわず covers all cases comprehensively, meaning 'not just X or Y, but everything'. It implies that something applies universally, not limited to the items named. More emphatic than も〜も.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"手といわず顔といわず、泥だらけになった。","reading":"てといわずかおといわず、どろだらけになった。","english":"His hands, face — everything was covered in mud."},{"japanese":"平日といわず休日といわず、働き続けた。","reading":"へいじつといわずきゅうじつといわず、はたらきつづけた。","english":"Whether weekdays or holidays, he kept working."},{"japanese":"男といわず女といわず、皆が参加した。","reading":"おとこといわずおんなといわず、みながさんかした。","english":"Whether men or women, everyone participated."}]

title: 〜とは裏腹に (contrary to / in contrast to)
pattern: N / S (plain) + とは裏腹に
meaning: Contrary to / in contrast to — reality is the opposite of what was expected or stated
explanation: とは裏腹に means the reality is ironically contrary to the stated expectation, appearance, or claim. Similar to に反して but carries a stronger sense of irony or unexpected reversal.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"笑顔とは裏腹に、心の中は悲しかった。","reading":"えがおとはうらはらに、こころのなかはかなしかった。","english":"Contrary to the smile, the heart was sad."},{"japanese":"外見とは裏腹に、とても優しい人です。","reading":"がいけんとはうらはらに、とてもやさしいひとです。","english":"Contrary to appearances, he is a very kind person."},{"japanese":"予想とは裏腹に、試験結果は悪かった。","reading":"よそうとはうらはらに、しけんけっかはわるかった。","english":"Contrary to expectations, the exam results were poor."}]

title: 〜ことのないよう (so that ... will not happen — preventive care)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + ことのないよう / ことのないように
meaning: So that ... will not happen — taking care to prevent an undesirable outcome
explanation: ことのないよう expresses that one takes care or prepares so that an undesirable event will not occur. More formal than ないように and often used in instructions, warnings, or careful planning contexts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"怪我のないよう、気をつけてください。","reading":"けがのないよう、きをつけてください。","english":"Please be careful so that no injuries occur."},{"japanese":"同じミスが起こることのないよう、対策を講じます。","reading":"おなじミスがおこることのないよう、たいさくをこうじます。","english":"I will take measures so that the same mistake will not occur again."},{"japanese":"不満が生じることのないよう、丁寧に対応します。","reading":"ふまんがしょうじることのないよう、ていねいにたいおうします。","english":"I will respond carefully so that no dissatisfaction arises."}]

title: 〜にひきかえ (in contrast to / unlike)
pattern: N + にひきかえ
meaning: In contrast to / unlike — sharp contrast between two things or people
explanation: にひきかえ expresses a sharp contrast between two things, often with an evaluative nuance. It highlights that Y is notably different (usually better or worse) compared to X. More emphatic than に比べて or 一方で.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"兄にひきかえ、弟はよく勉強します。","reading":"あににひきかえ、おとうとはよくべんきょうします。","english":"In contrast to his older brother, the younger brother studies a lot."},{"japanese":"去年にひきかえ、今年は天気がいいですね。","reading":"きょねんにひきかえ、ことしはてんきがいいですね。","english":"In contrast to last year, the weather is nice this year."},{"japanese":"あの人にひきかえ、あなたは本当に誠実ですね。","reading":"あのひとにひきかえ、あなたはほんとうにせいじつですね。","english":"In contrast to that person, you are truly sincere."}]

title: 〜ないではすまない (won't do without / obligated)
pattern: V-ない + ではすまない
meaning: Won't be resolved without / obligated — situation demands the action be done
explanation: ないではすまない expresses that the situation demands the action; without it, things will not be settled or resolved properly. Social, moral, or situational pressure compels the action. More formal than なければならない and emphasises the external pressure.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"こんな大きなミスは謝らないではすまない。","reading":"こんなおおきなミスはあやまらないではすまない。","english":"For such a large mistake, it won't do without an apology."},{"japanese":"彼が傷ついたなら、説明しないではすまないだろう。","reading":"かれがきずついたなら、せつめいしないではすまないだろう。","english":"If he was hurt, it won't do without an explanation."},{"japanese":"規則を破ったからには、何らかの責任を取らないではすまない。","reading":"きそくをやぶったからには、なんらかのせきにんをとらないではすまない。","english":"Having broken the rules, it won't do without taking some form of responsibility."}]

title: 〜いかんを問わず (regardless of — very formal)
pattern: N + のいかんを問わず
meaning: Regardless of / irrespective of — very formal absolute non-condition
explanation: いかんを問わず is the most formal way to say 'regardless of'. 問わず adds 'without questioning', making it absolute. Used in official policies, proclamations, and formal documents. More emphatic than にかかわらず.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"理由のいかんを問わず、遅刻は認められません。","reading":"りゆうのいかんをとわず、ちこくはみとめられません。","english":"Regardless of the reason, tardiness is not permitted."},{"japanese":"性別のいかんを問わず、全員が参加できます。","reading":"せいべつのいかんをとわず、ぜんいんがさんかできます。","english":"Regardless of gender, everyone can participate."},{"japanese":"国籍のいかんを問わず、応募を受け付けます。","reading":"こくせきのいかんをとわず、おうぼをうけつけます。","english":"Applications will be accepted regardless of nationality."}]

title: 〜にかかわる (to involve / to be at stake)
pattern: N + にかかわる + N
meaning: Involving / at stake / concerning — marks something as vitally related to
explanation: にかかわる as an attributive verb means 'involving / concerning / that affects'. It indicates the noun is implicated or at stake. 命にかかわる = life-threatening; 名誉にかかわる = involving one's honour. Used in serious contexts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"これは命にかかわる問題です。","reading":"これはいのちにかかわるもんだいです。","english":"This is a matter that involves life and death."},{"japanese":"会社の信頼にかかわる不祥事が発覚した。","reading":"かいしゃのしんらいにかかわるふしょうじがはっかくした。","english":"A scandal involving the company's trust was uncovered."},{"japanese":"名誉にかかわる問題は慎重に扱うべきだ。","reading":"めいよにかかわるもんだいはしんちょうにあつかうべきだ。","english":"Matters involving one's honour should be handled carefully."}]

title: 〜に事欠かない (never short of / there is no lack of)
pattern: N + に事欠かない
meaning: Never lacking in / there is no shortage of — something is abundantly present
explanation: に事欠かない means 'there is no shortage of X' or 'X is abundantly available'. Often used when there are more examples, opportunities, or instances than needed. Can be used with a slightly sarcastic tone when referring to problems.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"話題に事欠かない賑やかな場所です。","reading":"わだいにことかかないにぎやかなばしょです。","english":"It is a lively place that is never short of topics of conversation."},{"japanese":"問題に事欠かない職場で働いています。","reading":"もんだいにことかかないしょくばではたらいています。","english":"I work in a workplace that never lacks for problems."},{"japanese":"この街は観光スポットに事欠きません。","reading":"このまちはかんこうスポットにことかきません。","english":"This town never lacks tourist attractions."}]

title: 〜にかたくない (not hard to / easy to imagine/understand)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + にかたくない
meaning: Not hard to / easy to — something is easily imaginable or understandable
explanation: にかたくない (not difficult to) expresses that an action (usually imagining, understanding, or guessing) is easy given the circumstances. Common pattern: 想像にかたくない = not hard to imagine. Formal and literary.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼の苦しみは想像にかたくない。","reading":"かれのくるしみはそうぞうにかたくない。","english":"His suffering is not hard to imagine."},{"japanese":"その結果が失望をもたらしたことは理解にかたくない。","reading":"そのけっかがしつぼうをもたらしたことはりかいにかたくない。","english":"It is not hard to understand that the result brought disappointment."},{"japanese":"合格した彼女の喜びは察するにかたくない。","reading":"ごうかくしたかのじょのよろこびはさっするにかたくない。","english":"Her joy at passing is not hard to sense."}]

title: 〜だに (even — emphasis on a minimal case)
pattern: N + だに / V-stem + だに
meaning: Even / just the thought of — formal emphasis that even the minimal degree is significant
explanation: だに is a classical/formal particle meaning 'even'. 考えるだに恐ろしい = even the thought of it is frightening. It marks a minimal degree that is still sufficient to trigger the following reaction. Literary and rarely conversational.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"考えるだに恐ろしい出来事があった。","reading":"かんがえるだにおそろしいできごとがあった。","english":"There was an event that was frightening even to think about."},{"japanese":"想像するだに背筋が寒くなります。","reading":"そうぞうするだにせすじがさむくなります。","english":"Even imagining it sends a chill down my spine."},{"japanese":"その恐怖はいまだに思い出すだに震えが来る。","reading":"そのきょうふはいまだにおもいだすだにふるえがくる。","english":"That fear — even now, just recalling it makes me tremble."}]

title: 〜ては〜ては (repeatedly doing — cycle of actions)
pattern: V-て + は + V-て + は
meaning: Doing X and Y repeatedly — describes a repeated cycle of actions
explanation: ては〜ては describes a recurring pattern of two alternating actions. It can also express a repeated problematic cycle (失敗しては立ち上がる = failing and getting back up, over and over). Emphasises the repetitive nature.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"書いては消し、書いては消しを繰り返した。","reading":"かいてはけし、かいてはけしをくりかえした。","english":"I kept writing and erasing, writing and erasing."},{"japanese":"転んでは立ち上がり、何度も挑戦しました。","reading":"ころんではたちあがり、なんどもちょうせんしました。","english":"Falling and getting back up, I challenged again and again."},{"japanese":"読んでは考え、考えては読むという学び方です。","reading":"よんではかんがえ、かんがえてはよむというまなびかたです。","english":"It is a learning style of reading and thinking, thinking and reading."}]

title: 〜を禁じ得ない (cannot suppress / cannot help feeling)
pattern: N + を禁じ得ない / V-stem + を禁じ得ない
meaning: Cannot suppress / cannot help but feel — an emotion that arises unstoppably
explanation: を禁じ得ない means the speaker cannot suppress or hold back the emotion named. More formal and literary than ずにはいられない for emotions. Common with 涙 (tears), 怒り (anger), 同情 (sympathy), 驚き (surprise).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"その話を聞いて、涙を禁じ得ませんでした。","reading":"そのはなしをきいて、なみだをきんじえませんでした。","english":"Hearing that story, I could not suppress my tears."},{"japanese":"彼の態度には怒りを禁じ得ない。","reading":"かれのたいどにはいかりをきんじえない。","english":"I cannot suppress my anger at his attitude."},{"japanese":"その惨状には同情を禁じ得ませんでした。","reading":"そのさんじょうにはどうじょうをきんじえませんでした。","english":"I could not help but feel sympathy for that tragic situation."}]

title: 〜をかわきりに (beginning with — first triggering event in a series)
pattern: N + をかわきりに(して)
meaning: Starting with / beginning from — the first event that triggered a series
explanation: をかわきりに marks the opening act that set off a chain of events. Same as を皮切りに but written differently (both are read をかわきりに). Used in formal and journalistic contexts to describe momentum building from a starting point.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼のスピーチをかわきりに、各国の代表が発言した。","reading":"かれのスピーチをかわきりに、かっこくのだいひょうがはつげんした。","english":"Starting with his speech, representatives from each country began to speak."},{"japanese":"一人の抗議をかわきりに、大きなデモが始まった。","reading":"ひとりのこうぎをかわきりに、おおきなデモがはじまった。","english":"Starting with one person's protest, a large demonstration began."},{"japanese":"この発見をかわきりにして、研究が加速した。","reading":"このはっけんをかわきりにして、けんきゅうがかそくした。","english":"Starting from this discovery, research accelerated."}]

title: 〜ことだから (given that it's ... / knowing ...)
pattern: N + のことだから
meaning: Given that it's [person/thing] / knowing [their nature] — inference based on known character
explanation: のことだから (usually with a person's name or character type) means 'given that it's [person], it's natural to expect...'. The speaker reasons based on what they know about the person's character or the nature of the thing. Familiar and confident inference.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"田中さんのことだから、きっとうまくやるでしょう。","reading":"たなかさんのことだから、きっとうまくやるでしょう。","english":"Knowing Tanaka-san, he will surely handle it well."},{"japanese":"彼のことだから、約束の時間には絶対に来ると思います。","reading":"かれのことだから、やくそくのじかんにはぜったいにくるとおもいます。","english":"Knowing him, I'm sure he'll come at the promised time."},{"japanese":"この店のことだから、品質は保証できます。","reading":"このみせのことだから、ひんしつはほしょうできます。","english":"Knowing this shop, I can guarantee the quality."}]

title: 〜べくもない (there is no way / cannot possibly)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + べくもない
meaning: There is no way to / cannot possibly — complete impossibility of action or outcome
explanation: べくもない is a formal/literary expression for impossibility. 知るべくもない = there is no way to know; 想像するべくもない = it cannot possibly be imagined. More literary than できない or ようがない.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"当時の状況は想像するべくもありません。","reading":"とうじのじょうきょうはそうぞうするべくもありません。","english":"There is no way to imagine the situation at that time."},{"japanese":"真実は私たちには知るべくもない。","reading":"しんじつはわたしたちにはしるべくもない。","english":"The truth is something we have no way of knowing."},{"japanese":"そのような才能は凡人には持つべくもない。","reading":"そのようなさいのうはぼんじんにはもつべくもない。","english":"Such talent is something ordinary people cannot possibly possess."}]

title: 〜なくして(は) (without ... it is impossible)
pattern: N + なくして(は) / V-ない + なくして(は)
meaning: Without — formal assertion that something is absolutely indispensable
explanation: なくして(は) is the formal literary equivalent of なしに(は), meaning 'without X, Y is impossible'. The は adds a conditional sense. More emphatic and literary than なしに. Often used in speeches and formal writing.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"信頼なくして、友情は成り立たない。","reading":"しんらいなくして、ゆうじょうはなりたたない。","english":"Without trust, friendship cannot exist."},{"japanese":"努力なくして、成功はあり得ない。","reading":"どりょくなくして、せいこうはありえない。","english":"Without effort, success is impossible."},{"japanese":"お互いの理解なくしては、解決できません。","reading":"おたがいのりかいなくしては、かいけつできません。","english":"Without mutual understanding, there can be no resolution."}]

title: 〜に相まって (combined with / together with — synergistic)
pattern: N + に相まって
meaning: Combined with / along with — two positive elements combine to produce a stronger effect
explanation: に相まって expresses that two factors combine or interact to produce an enhanced effect. Both elements reinforce each other. Used in formal and journalistic contexts to describe synergistic combinations.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"才能に相まって、努力が実を結びました。","reading":"さいのうにあいまって、どりょくがみをむすびました。","english":"Combined with talent, effort bore fruit."},{"japanese":"暖かい気候に相まって、農業が盛んです。","reading":"あたたかいきこうにあいまって、のうぎょうがさかんです。","english":"Combined with the warm climate, agriculture is thriving."},{"japanese":"SNSの普及に相まって、情報の拡散が速くなっています。","reading":"SNSのふきゅうにあいまって、じょうほうのかくさんがはやくなっています。","english":"Combined with the spread of social media, information is spreading more quickly."}]

title: 〜ずとも (even without / even if not)
pattern: V-ない stem + ずとも
meaning: Even without / even if not — formal; even if the condition doesn't hold, the result follows
explanation: ずとも is the formal/literary equivalent of なくても (even without). 言わずとも = even without saying; 見ずとも = even without seeing. Common in set expressions and literary contexts. する → せずとも.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"言わずとも、彼には分かっていると思います。","reading":"いわずとも、かれにはわかっているとおもいます。","english":"Even without saying it, I think he already knows."},{"japanese":"急がずとも、時間は十分にあります。","reading":"いそがずとも、じかんはじゅうぶんにあります。","english":"Even without hurrying, there is plenty of time."},{"japanese":"説明せずとも、状況は明らかです。","reading":"せつめいせずとも、じょうきょうはあきらかです。","english":"Even without explanation, the situation is clear."}]

title: 〜よしもない (there is no way to / no means to)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + よしもない
meaning: There is no way to / no means of — complete impossibility from lack of means
explanation: よしもない (also: よしがない) is a formal/literary expression meaning 'there is no means / no way'. Similar to ようがない but more literary. 知るよしもない = there is no way of knowing. Yoshimono as 'means/method'.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"真相を知るよしもなかった。","reading":"しんそうをしるよしもなかった。","english":"There was no way to know the truth."},{"japanese":"彼がどこにいるか、今となっては知るよしもない。","reading":"かれがどこにいるか、いまとなってはしるよしもない。","english":"Where he is now, there is no way to know at this point."},{"japanese":"当時は将来がどうなるか、知るよしもなかった。","reading":"とうじはしょうらいがどうなるか、しるよしもなかった。","english":"At that time, there was no way of knowing what the future would hold."}]

title: 〜すら (even — formal emphasis on extreme case)
pattern: N + すら / V-stem + すら
meaning: Even — marks an extreme or surprising case; formal equivalent of さえ
explanation: すら is a formal/literary particle meaning 'even', equivalent to casual さえ. It marks a surprising or extreme case: if even this (the minimum or most surprising) is true, how much more so others. Used in formal and literary writing.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼は自分の名前すら書けない状態だった。","reading":"かれはじぶんのなまえすらかけないじょうたいだった。","english":"He was in a state where he couldn't even write his own name."},{"japanese":"専門家すら解けない問題です。","reading":"せんもんかすらとけないもんだいです。","english":"It is a problem that even experts cannot solve."},{"japanese":"一瞬すら気を抜けない状況が続いています。","reading":"いっしゅんすらきをぬけないじょうきょうがつづいています。","english":"A situation where I cannot let my guard down even for an instant continues."}]
